name: Charged lepton flavor violation: mu -> e gamma
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict Br(mu+ -> e+ gamma) below the MEG experiment upper limit. In the SM with massless neutrinos, charged lepton flavor violation (CLFV) is exactly forbidden; even with neutrino masses, the predicted branching is ~10^-54, completely negligible. Any observable CLFV is unambiguous BSM physics.

regime.description: Muon-scale physics; loop sensitivity to multi-TeV BSM masses depending on couplings.

current_bound.reference: Baldini et al. (MEG Collaboration), 'Search for the lepton flavour violating decay mu+ -> e+ gamma with the full dataset of the MEG experiment', EPJC 76, 434 (2016), arXiv:1605.05081

what_it_excludes: Severely constrains supersymmetric models with non-trivial slepton mixing, leptoquark models, and any TeV-scale flavor structure that mixes muon and electron lepton numbers. MEG II (running) targets ~6 x 10^-14, an order of magnitude improvement.

parameterization.name: SMEFT lepton-flavor-violating dipole operators C_eB^(12) and C_eW^(12) (mu-e off-diagonal), plus four-fermion LFV operators contributing through penguins.
parameterization.reference: Crivellin, Najjari, Rosiek, JHEP 04, 167 (2014), arXiv:1312.0634; Cirigliano, Davidson, Kuno, PLB 771, 242 (2017).
parameterization.summary: Br(mu -> e gamma) = (3 alpha / pi) * |C_egamma^(12) v / Lambda^2|^2 / (G_F^2 m_mu^2).

framework_inputs: Match the framework onto SMEFT and read off the off-diagonal dipole Wilson coefficients C_eB^(12), C_eW^(12).
Include possible Z-penguin and box contributions for predicting other CLFV channels (mu-e conversion, mu -> 3e).

primary_references.citation: Baldini et al. (MEG), 'Search for the lepton flavour violating decay mu+ -> e+ gamma with the full dataset of the MEG experiment', EPJC 76, 434 (2016)
Calibbi and Signorelli, 'Charged Lepton Flavour Violation: An Experimental and Theoretical Introduction', Riv. Nuovo Cim. 41, 71 (2018)
